L1.area: Social authentication and OAuth flow management

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where a URL parameter controlling an OAuth redirect destination is passed directly to a browser navigation call without validation or sanitization. An attacker can craft a URL with a malicious redirect value using a dangerous protocol scheme such as javascript:, which will be executed in the user's browser context when processed.

L3.area: OAuth callback response processing and redirect URL navigation
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the OAuth callback response processing logic where a URL parameter controlling the redirect destination is decoded and passed directly to a browser navigation assignment without URL scheme validation. An attacker can supply a javascript: protocol URI as the redirect destination, causing arbitrary script execution in the user's browser when the OAuth callback is processed.

ground_truth.reason: The oauth_redirect URL parameter is decoded and passed directly to location.assign() without any validation, allowing an attacker to inject a javascript: URI and execute arbitrary scripts in the user's browser.
